reaches.name: East Walker River — 1/4 mile above Sweetwater Creek confluence downstream to 1/2 mile below Red Wash Creek confluence (catch-and-release)
reaches.fromDesc: 1/4 mile above the confluence of the East Walker River and Sweetwater Creek
reaches.toDesc: 1/2 mile below the confluence of the East Walker River and Red Wash Creek

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: East Walker River: Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Except in that portion of the East Walker River which is from one-quarter mile above the confluence of the East Walker River and Sweetwater Creek downstream to one-half mile below the confluence of the East Walker River and Red Wash Creek which is catch and release fishing only. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Walker River, East Fork — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: The East Walker River is situated in Lyon County, Nevada, flowing through Lyon County originating along the eastern slope of the Sierra Nevada (California)... Rainbow trout, Brown trout, Mountain whitefish, Cutthroat trout (historically).

sources.title: Nev. Admin. Code § 503.504(3)(b)(4) — Western Region: Applicable waters; restrictions on bait and lures
sources.quotedText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used in: ... The portion of the East Walker River which is from one-quarter of a mile above the confluence of the East Walker River and Sweetwater Creek downstream to one-half of a mile below the confluence of the East Walker River and Red Wash Creek.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Open year-round; the special reach from 1/4 mile above the Sweetwater Creek confluence downstream to 1/2 mile below the Red Wash Creek confluence is catch-and-release only (see reach-scoped rules).

regulations.humanSummary: East Walker River is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit is 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 5 may be black bass. Possession limit is 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 10 may be black bass. This bag rule applies outside the catch-and-release reach (see reach-scoped rule below).

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish (no more than 5 black bass). Possession limit: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (no more than 10 black bass). Does not apply within the catch-and-release reach.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass. ... Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass.

parameters.counting_rule: No fish of any species may be kept within this reach; all fish caught must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: From 1/4 mile above the Sweetwater Creek confluence downstream to 1/2 mile below the Red Wash Creek confluence, the East Walker River is catch-and-release only — no fish may be kept.
regulations.verbatimText: Except in that portion of the East Walker River which is from one-quarter mile above the confluence of the East Walker River and Sweetwater Creek downstream to one-half mile below the confluence of the East Walker River and Red Wash Creek which is catch and release fishing only.

regulations.humanSummary: In this reach, only artificial lures with a single barbless hook may be used (no bait).
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with single barbless hooks may be used in: ... The portion of the East Walker River which is from one-quarter of a mile above the confluence of the East Walker River and Sweetwater Creek downstream to one-half of a mile below the confluence of the East Walker River and Red Wash Creek.

parameters.note: No hour-of-day restriction; fishing permitted any hour of the day or night.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
